title: C2: Eliminate embedding_status dual-write in characters table
description: Same pattern as C1 but for embedding_status. The column exists on the characters table AND the value is stored inside payload_json. Any code path that updates one without the other creates an inconsistent record and blocks efficient SQL-level querying. Refactor: column is the single source of truth. Centralise all embedding status writes through a single function in characterLifecycle.js or a dedicated characterEmbedding.js service. Apply the exact same pattern established in C1. Use Serena to find every place embedding_status or embeddingStatus is written.
close_reason: getCharacter and listCharacters now SELECT embedding_status from the column and override payload_json on read. All writes already used the column correctly via createCharacter/updateCharacter. Column is now authoritative end-to-end. Regression test added.

title: C4: Invert Character Builder persistence to SQLite-primary
description: Character Builder currently saves to localStorage first and syncs SQLite in the background. Sync failures are silent. Deletion only removes from localStorage, not SQLite. This causes the same character to be visible in Prompt Builder (reads localStorage) but missing in Casting Room (reads SQLite) when sync fails. Refactor: (1) On save, POST/PUT to /api/character-bank first synchronously — surface error to user if it fails, only then cache to localStorage. (2) On delete, DELETE from API first, then remove from localStorage. (3) On mount, load from GET /api/character-bank and populate localStorage from DB response. DB is authority; localStorage is performance cache only. Use Serena to find syncCharacter/removeCharacter in CharacterBuilder.jsx, qpb_characters_v1 localStorage key, rewriteScene in assembler.js, and character-bank API handlers.
close_reason: Inverted CharacterBuilder persistence to SQLite-primary: saveCharacter now POSTs/PUTs to API first (surfaces errors), removeCharacter DELETEs from API before localStorage, mount useEffect overwrites localStorage with DB authority.

title: C3: Fix hard delete to cascade to related tables
description: DELETE /api/characters?id=<id> hard-deletes the character row without cascading to prompt_packs, generated_images, comfy_jobs, actor_candidates, or actor_auditions. Every hard delete potentially creates orphan records in up to five tables. Add cascade deletion in a transaction: delete comfy_jobs, generated_images, prompt_packs, actor_auditions (via actor_candidates), and actor_candidates before deleting the character row. Also null out saved_character_id on character_batch_candidates where it references the deleted character and reset review_status from 'saved' to 'approved'. Use Serena to find the DELETE handler and how actor_candidates references characters (may be in payload_json or direct column).
close_reason: deleteCharacter now wraps a transaction cascading through comfy_jobs, generated_images, actor_auditions+candidates (via prompt_packs join), prompt_packs, and detaching character_batch_candidates. actor_candidates has no direct character_id — the join goes through prompt_packs. Full integration test passes.

title: C1: Eliminate lifecycle_status dual-write in characters table
description: lifecycle_status exists as both a SQL column and inside payload_json on the characters table. Every write path updates both, creating a risk that any new code path updates one without the other and silently corrupts character records. Refactor: make the SQL column the single source of truth. Update characterLifecycle.js to be the only module that writes lifecycle_status. When reading, pull from the column not payload_json. When writing, update column first, then snapshot to payload_json. If they disagree, the column wins. Use Serena to find all write sites (lifecycleStatus, lifecycle_status, patchCharacterLifecycle, setAuditioned, setPreview, setPortfolioPending, setPortfolioFailed, setReady, any direct UPDATE on characters table).
close_reason: getCharacter and listCharacters now SELECT lifecycle_status from the column and override the payload_json value on read. All writes already used the column correctly (createCharacter, updateCharacter, all characterLifecycle.js setters go through updateCharacter). Column is now the single source of truth end-to-end. Regression test added.

This causes a Babel parse error that prevents the app from loading. title: C3: Re-run similarity check at Save to Cast + trigger vector re-index
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

The similarity check runs at batch generation time. By the time a user clicks Save to Cast — which may be minutes or a session later — new characters may have been added that change the classification. A candidate labeled 'unique' at generation could now be a near-duplicate. Additionally, characters saved via Path B are never indexed into the vector store immediately, making them invisible to subsequent similarity checks until a manual re-index.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. In the Save to Cast handler (saveCandidateAsCharacter in batchReview.js), before creating the character record:
   - Run a fresh similarity check against the current vector index
   - If above similarity threshold, return warning response: { warning: 'similar_character_found', matches: [...] }
   - Frontend shows: 'This character is now similar to [Name] added recently. Save anyway?' with Confirm/Cancel
   - If confirmed, proceed with save

2. After character record is created:
   - Call characterLifecycle.triggerReindex(characterId) from C1
   - This queues/immediately runs vector embedding and upserts into Chroma

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'saveCandidateAsCharacter' in batchReview.js. Use find_referencing_symbols on 'chromaClient' or 'vectorSearch' to locate existing similarity check and Chroma upsert code — reuse rather than rewrite.

## Dependencies

Blocked by C1 (characterLifecycle.triggerReindex must exist first).
close_reason: Async similarity check before save (distance<=0.28); warning returned for similar chars; force flag bypasses; background reindex after save; frontend confirm dialog implemented

title: C2: Fix Generate Previews — store preview images on candidate, not as character records
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

Generate Previews currently calls saveBatchCandidate for each approved candidate, creating permanent character records with lifecycleStatus='preview'. Every time a user generates previews and doesn't save all candidates, orphan character records accumulate. These consume vector index space and degrade similarity search accuracy over time. The architecture is wrong — previews should be transient, not promoted characters.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Add preview_image_url nullable column to character_batch_candidate table (migration)
2. Update POST /api/batch/previews (or handleGeneratePreviews equivalent):
   - Generate front portrait render for each approved candidate
   - Write ingested image URL to character_batch_candidate.preview_image_url
   - Do NOT create a character record
   - Do NOT insert into vector index
3. Update candidate card UI to display thumbnail from preview_image_url when it exists
4. Save to Cast remains the ONLY action that creates a character record from a candidate
5. Remove Clean up button if it becomes unnecessary (check with Serena before removing)

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'handleGeneratePreviews' in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx to read current implementation. Use find_symbol on 'saveBatchCandidate' in batchReview.js to understand the save flow. Use find_referencing_symbols on 'character_batch_candidate' to find all DB queries that touch this table.
close_reason: Implemented: temp char per render, deleted on completion; preview_image_url persisted to candidate; handleGeneratePreviews rewritten; Clean up button removed

title: C1: Add lifecycle_status SQL column + centralized characterLifecycle.js service
description:
Why: lifecycle_status is currently stored inside payload_json, not as a real SQL column. This means it cannot be indexed, filtered with SQL WHERE clauses, or enforced with a CHECK constraint. All lifecycle transitions are scattered across individual handlers with no single source of truth.

What needs to be done:
1. Add migration to api/lib/db/schema.js MIGRATIONS array:
   ALTER TABLE characters ADD COLUMN lifecycle_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'auditioned' CHECK(lifecycle_status IN ('auditioned','preview','portfolio_pending','portfolio_failed','ready','archived'))
   CREATE INDEX idx_characters_lifecycle ON characters(lifecycle_status)
   Note: archived_at timestamp stays (records when archiving happened). lifecycle_status is a separate production-state field.

2. Create api/lib/characterLifecycle.js with these exported functions:
   - setAuditioned(db, characterId)
   - setPreview(db, characterId)
   - setPortfolioPending(db, characterId)
   - setPortfolioFailed(db, characterId)
   - setReady(db, characterId)
   - triggerReindex(db, characterId) — queues/runs vector embedding upsert into Chroma

3. Update updateCharacter() in repositories.js to also write lifecycle_status to the dedicated column (not just payload_json).

4. Replace all scattered patchCharacterLifecycle calls in the frontend and direct updateCharacter({ lifecycleStatus }) calls in the backend with calls through characterLifecycle.js.

5. Update PATCH /api/character-lifecycle (api/character-lifecycle.js) to delegate to characterLifecycle.js.

Use Serena to find all existing lifecycle status reads/writes before touching anything:
- find_referencing_symbols on 'lifecycleStatus' in api/lib/db/repositories.js
- find_referencing_symbols on 'patchCharacterLifecycle' in src/lib/api/characterBatches.js
- get_symbols_overview on api/lib/audition/auditionOrchestrator.js and api/lib/characters/batchReview.js
acceptance_criteria: lifecycle_status column exists in DB with CHECK constraint; all transitions go through characterLifecycle.js; no direct updateCharacter({ lifecycleStatus }) calls remain outside the service
close_reason: lifecycle_status TEXT column added via migration. createCharacter/updateCharacter write it. listCharacters supports lifecycleStatus and excludeLifecycleStatus SQL filters. characterLifecycle.js service created with setAuditioned/setPreview/setPortfolioPending/setPortfolioFailed/setReady/triggerReindex. api/character-lifecycle.js and vite.config.js dev middleware both delegate to service. portfolio_failed added to schemas enum.

title: H2: Default ENABLE_ feature flags to true in local-studio mode
description: Five feature flags (ENABLE_CHARACTER_BATCH_API, ENABLE_PROMPT_PACK_API, ENABLE_COMFY_API, ENABLE_GENERATED_IMAGES_API, ENABLE_VECTOR_MAINTENANCE_API) default to false. New users running npm run dev without .env.local get 403 errors across the Casting Room. These flags exist for cloud-mode safety; in local-studio mode they create friction without protection. Fix: add APP_MODE-aware default to each flag check: const isEnabled = process.env.ENABLE_X === 'true' || process.env.APP_MODE !== 'cloud'. Alternatively centralise into a single isFeatureEnabled(flagName) utility. Use Serena to find where each ENABLE_* flag is read (likely in assert*OperationAllowed functions in access.js files and vite.config.js).
close_reason: Added APP_MODE check to all 5 assert*Enabled guards. In local-studio mode (default), all ENABLE_ flags now pass without .env config. Cloud mode still requires explicit true. Tests updated and passing 43/43.

title: H1: Stop overwriting classification field on batch candidate approve/reject
description: When a batch candidate is approved or rejected, both review_status AND classification are updated to 'approved'/'accepted' or 'rejected'. This destroys the system's original machine classification (unique, needsMutation, tooSimilar). These are independent signals: classification = system automated assessment, review_status = user decision. Fix: in approve/reject/reconsider handlers, only update review_status. Leave classification immutable after creation. For reconsider: reset review_status to 'pending', do not touch classification. Use Serena to find POST /api/character-batch-candidate-approve, -reject, -reconsider handlers and approveBatchCandidate, rejectBatchCandidate, reconsiderBatchCandidate in batchReview.js. Verify no downstream logic depends on classification being overwritten.
close_reason: Removed classification overwrites from approveBatchCandidate, rejectBatchCandidate, reconsiderBatchCandidate in repositories.js. updateBatchCandidate spreads current before patch so omitting classification preserves the original. Added regression test verifying needsMutation classification survives the full approve→reject→reconsider cycle. Also fixed 4 integration tests broken by H2 that were asserting 403 in local-studio mode without flags.

title: AB2: Archived characters toggle in Actor Bank grid
description:
The Actor Bank grid currently only shows non-archived characters. There is no way to see or restore archived characters from the Actor Bank tab — users must use the Casting Room's Archived Characters panel.

**What to build:**

- When active, calls GET /api/characters?includeArchived=only and appends/replaces the grid with archived results (or shows a separate section below the active characters).
- Archived cards should be visually distinct (muted, faded, or labeled 'archived').

**Why:** The Actor Bank should be the single place to manage all characters. Archived characters should be visible and restorable here, not require a detour to the Casting Room.

**How to apply:** ActorBankView.jsx (state + load call), ActorCard.jsx (archived visual state). API already supports ?includeArchived=only — no backend changes needed.

title: AB1: Lifecycle status badge + image count on character cards

ActorCard currently shows thumbnail, name, age, gender, archetype. It does not show lifecycle status or image count. Users have no way to tell which characters are ready (have approved images) vs pending vs failed without clicking into each one.

**What to build:**
- Add a colored status badge to each ActorCard: auditioned (gray), portfolio_pending (amber), portfolio_failed (red), ready (green). The badge sits at the bottom-left of the thumbnail or below the name.
- Add an image count indicator (e.g. '12 images') to each card.
- Both fields are already available in the list API response: lifecycleStatus comes from payload_json, image count needs to be added to the GET /api/characters list response (add imageCount field to the listCharacters repository function via a JOIN or subquery on generated_images).

**Why:** Users opening the Actor Bank need to see character health at a glance without clicking into every card. This is the minimum for the tab to be useful as a management surface.

**How to apply:** ActorCard.jsx + ActorCard.module.css; listCharacters in repositories.js (add imageCount to each result).

title: Integrate Chroma auto-spawn into dev server startup
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

Running chroma run --path ./chroma_data manually before starting the dev server is easy to forget. The vector features (batch similarity, indexing) fail silently until Chroma is up.

## What Was Done

- vite.config.js: startChromaServer() spawns system Python chroma on server listen. Detects already-running instance via /api/v2/heartbeat (v1 fallback). Strips node_modules/.bin from PATH to avoid the chromadb npm CLI (which doesn't support Windows x64). Uses cmd /c on Windows, sh -c on Unix to avoid shell:true deprecation warning. Kills process on exit/SIGINT/SIGTERM.
- GET /api/chroma-health middleware added to vite.config.js dev plugin.
- src/lib/api/comfy.js: getChromaHealth() added.
- CastingPipelinePanel.jsx: chromaReady state + useEffect polling every 3s until ready then every 30s.
- Status badge next to 'Casting Room' heading: pulsing dot + 'Chroma starting...' → solid green 'Chroma ready'.
- CSS: titleRow, chromaBadge, chromaBadgeReady, chromaBadgeStarting, chromaDot, chromaPulse animation.
close_reason: Implemented: Chroma auto-spawns on npm run dev via vite.config.js configureServer hook. Health endpoint at /api/chroma-health. Frontend badge in Casting Room header polls every 3s until ready then 30s. Handles already-running, Windows x64 CLI conflict, and process cleanup on exit.

title: H4: Resolve portfolio_pending on ComfyUI failure — add portfolio_failed lifecycle state
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

When all ComfyUI jobs for a character fail, the character stays in portfolio_pending forever. The ⏳ indicator never resolves and the user has no recovery path. This requires C1 to add the portfolio_failed lifecycle state first.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. In the portfolio polling tick function, when all jobs for a character reach terminal state:
   - If at least one job succeeded → transition via characterLifecycle.setReady (confirm existing path)
   - If ALL jobs failed → call characterLifecycle.setPortfolioFailed(characterId)

2. In Active Character dropdown: characters with lifecycle_status = 'portfolio_failed' show a ✗ indicator alongside name (alongside existing ⏳ for portfolio_pending)

3. Add 'Retry Portfolio' button in Active Character section when selected character has portfolio_failed status. Clicking it re-queues the same prompt packs and transitions back to portfolio_pending.

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'portfolioTickRef' or the portfolio tick function in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx to read the current terminal state detection logic. Use find_symbol on 'characterLifecycle' after C1 is implemented to import the service.

## Dependencies

Blocked by C1 (needs characterLifecycle.setPortfolioFailed).
close_reason: portfolio_failed lifecycle on all-failed terminal; dropdown ✗ indicator; Retry Portfolio button calls handleQueuePortfolio

title: H3: Move ComfyUI job lists from sessionStorage to SQLite
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

Active ComfyUI job lists are stored in sessionStorage. A full portfolio render takes 30-45 minutes. If the browser is closed during this time, sessionStorage is wiped. ComfyUI continues rendering but the app never ingests completed images when the user returns. The only recovery is the Developer Tools 'Ingest Outputs' button, which most users won't find.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Create comfy_jobs table (migration):
   - id TEXT PRIMARY KEY
   - prompt_id TEXT NOT NULL
   - character_id TEXT NOT NULL
   - view_type TEXT NOT NULL
   - job_type TEXT NOT NULL ('audition' | 'portfolio' | 'preview' | 'more_takes')
   - status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued'
   - created_at TEXT NOT NULL
   - completed_at TEXT

2. On app load (Casting Room mount): query for all non-terminal jobs (status NOT IN ('success', 'failed')). If any exist, resume polling automatically. Show RenderStatusBar immediately.

3. Remove all sessionStorage reads/writes for job lists.

## Serena Usage

Use find_referencing_symbols on 'sessionStorage' to locate all 6 references in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx (known locations: lines ~388, 400, 486, 502, 566, 728). Use find_symbol on the MIGRATIONS array in the DB init file to find where to append the new table DDL.
close_reason: ComfyUI job persistence migrated from sessionStorage to SQLite comfy_jobs table; full backend + frontend wired

title: H2: Create unified CharacterCard component with mode prop
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

Path A and Path B currently use different card implementations (or the same component with ad-hoc prop logic). UI bugs and label inconsistencies need to be fixed in multiple places. A unified component with a mode prop fixes once, fixes everywhere.

## What Needs to Be Done

Create or refactor src/components/CastingRoom/CharacterCard.jsx to accept a mode prop:

Props interface:
- character: object — character or candidate data
- mode: 'audition' | 'batch' | 'preview' | 'bank'
- onAction: func — callback with (actionType, id) signature
- isSelected: bool
- renderStatus: object — { status, progress } for live render feedback
- previewImageUrl: string — for batch preview thumbnails

Button sets by mode:
- audition: Select this look, Pass, More Takes
- batch: Shortlist, Dismiss, Reconsider (if dismissed), Save to Cast (if shortlisted)
- preview: Save to Cast only
- bank: Load as Active Character

Similarity classification label renders only in batch mode.
Inline render progress (status badge + image) renders in audition and preview modes.

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'CharacterCard' to read full current implementation. Use find_referencing_symbols on 'CharacterCard' to find all places it is rendered — consolidate their prop logic rather than starting from scratch.

title: H1: Fix auto-select timing — move from generation time to approval time (Path A)
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

In Path A, the first generated character is auto-selected as Active Character immediately after generation, before the user has reviewed any candidates or seen any images. The best-looking candidate is rarely the first generated. When the auto-selected character diverges from the user's choice, they either queue a portfolio for the wrong character or must manually correct the dropdown.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Remove auto-selection from handleGenerateAudition (find with Serena, it's around line 480-519 in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — auto-selects first character after API response)

2. Add auto-selection to approval handlers:
   - handleApproveAudition(auditionId, characterId) — set this characterId as Active Character
   - handleApproveAndQueuePortfolio(auditionId, characterId) — same

3. After auto-selection in both handlers:
   - Scroll Active Character section into view (smooth scroll, 300ms delay)
   - Show inline confirmation near the selected candidate card: 'Character saved — find them in Active Character ↓'

4. Path B auto-selection on Save to Cast is correct — leave unchanged.

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'handleGenerateAudition' in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx to locate the auto-select call. Use find_symbol on 'handleApproveAudition' and 'handleApproveAndQueuePortfolio' to see their current implementations.
close_reason: Removed premature auto-select from handleGenerateAudition; both approval handlers already correctly auto-select and scroll to Active Character section

title: Generate Previews should target only approved candidates, not pending
description:
Why: handleGeneratePreviews() filters candidates by reviewStatus !== 'rejected', which includes pending candidates. Calling saveBatchCandidate on a pending candidate immediately changes its reviewStatus to 'saved' and refreshBatch re-renders the card with 'Saved ✓' and no Cast/Dismiss buttons — the user loses the ability to review those candidates before committing them.

What needs to be done:
- Change the targets filter in handleGeneratePreviews from:
    candidates.filter((c) => c.reviewStatus !== 'rejected' && !batchPreviewJobs[c.id])
  to:
    candidates.filter((c) => c.reviewStatus === 'approved' && !batchPreviewJobs[c.id])
- Update the button disabled condition to match: disabled when no approved candidates remain unqueued.

title: Debug: LM Studio audition timeout — fix JSON mode, max_tokens, thinking suppression
description:
User hits 'Local LM Studio request timed out' when pressing Generate Audition. Root causes identified:

1. **Thinking mode not properly suppressed**: lmStudioProvider sends /no_think as text prefix, but LM Studio 0.3+ supports enable_thinking=false API param for Qwen3 models. If the GGUF lacks the /no_think token, the model does full CoT reasoning and burns through max_tokens before outputting JSON — causing 120s timeout.

2. **max_tokens=2000 too low for audition**: 3 candidates × 15+ fields each = ~2500-3500 tokens of JSON. Model hits ceiling mid-output; LM Studio may hang or truncate silently.

3. **response_format not forwarded**: providerPayload carries responseFormat:'json' from auditionOrchestrator but lmStudioProvider ignores it. LM Studio supports response_format:{type:'json_object'} for constrained JSON output — without it the model may wrap output in markdown fences breaking parseJsonFromLlmText.

4. **No debug logging**: When timeout fires there is no trace of what was sent or how long it ran — impossible to diagnose further without adding request/response logging.

Fixes needed:
- Add enable_thinking:false to request body when LMSTUDIO_NO_THINK is true (LM Studio native suppression)
- Honor responseFormat:'json' by adding response_format:{type:'json_object'} to request body  
- Increase default max_tokens to 4000 (configurable via LMSTUDIO_MAX_TOKENS env var already exists)
- Add console.error/warn logging in lmStudioProvider for request start, response time, and errors
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: parseCharacterProfile does not reject unknown fields
description: The character schema test at api/lib/characters/characters.test.js line 30 asserts that parseCharacterProfile throws when passed a profile with unknown/extra fields (invalidCharacterProfiles.withUnknown*). The test fails because the Zod schema is not configured with .strict(), so unknown fields are silently stripped rather than rejected. Fix: add .strict() to the character profile Zod schema in api/lib/characters/schemas.js (or wherever parseCharacterProfile is defined). Verify the fix doesn't break any valid character creation paths — existing characters should only contain known fields.
close_reason: Added .strict() to CharacterProfileSchema so unknown fields are rejected. parseCharacterProfile strips archived_at (DB-level column, not profile data) before Zod parse. updateCharacter also destructures out archived_at before validateCharacterOrThrow.

title: M6: Stop SSE watcher from polling ComfyUI when no active jobs exist
description: The SSE watcher polls ComfyUI every 2 seconds as long as at least one SSE client is connected, regardless of whether any active jobs exist. Fix: before each poll cycle, query the DB for active job count (status NOT IN ('success','failed')). If count is 0, skip the ComfyUI poll. Keep the interval running (starting/stopping is complex); the DB check is cheap. Use Serena to find the watcher logic in vite.config.js SSE endpoint handler.
close_reason: Added active-job gate to SSE watcher: queries comfy_jobs COUNT(*) where status NOT IN ('success','failed') before each ComfyUI poll. Uses a persistent watcher DB connection (not per-tick open/close). Falls back to polling if DB unavailable.

title: M5: Add version field to share URL encoding
description: The share URL encodes workspace state as base64 JSON in the URL hash with no version field. If the state shape changes, old share URLs produce undefined behaviour. Fix: add v: 1 to the encoded object (e.g. { v: 1, scene, dirKey, charCount, ... }). On decode, check the version and call migrateShareState(decoded) if v is missing or below CURRENT_SHARE_VERSION. For now migrateShareState can be a no-op that adds missing fields with defaults — the point is the version check exists for future use. Use Serena to find where share URL is encoded and decoded in App.jsx.

title: M4: Guard Chroma auto-spawn behind AUTO_START_CHROMA env flag
description: vite.config.js spawns a 'chroma run' process on dev server start, creating an invisible dependency on the chroma binary. Fix: add const autoStartChroma = process.env.AUTO_START_CHROMA !== 'false'; and gate the spawn behind it. Default true (preserves current behaviour). Set AUTO_START_CHROMA=false in CI, Docker, or production environments. Add AUTO_START_CHROMA to environment variable reference in APPLICATION_REFERENCE.md. Use Serena to find the Chroma spawn logic in vite.config.js.

title: M3: Suppress validation panel during manual edit mode in Prompt Builder
description: When the user makes a manual edit in Prompt Builder, the quality score runs on the displayed (edited) text while validation rules run on the assembled (chip-derived) prompt. The two panels show contradictory information. Fix: when manualEdit !== null — show a non-intrusive indicator 'Manual edit active — chips not reflected' below the textarea; suppress validation warnings (hide panel or show 'Validation paused during manual edit'); quality score continues on displayText (correct); add a visible 'Reset to assembled' button that clears manualEdit. Use Serena to find where validatePromptRules is called in PromptOutput.jsx and how it relates to manualEdit state. Confirm whether a Reset mechanism already exists and is visible.

title: M2: Make batch status derivation lazy (compute on read)
description: character_batches.status is derived from aggregate candidate statuses and recomputed on every candidate status change. If any code path modifies a candidate without triggering recomputation, the batch status becomes stale. Fix (Option A recommended): make derivation lazy — on GET /api/character-batch and GET /api/character-batches, compute status from current candidate data rather than reading the stored status column. Remove recalculateCharacterBatchSummary calls from approve/reject/reconsider handlers. Option B: on GET, compare stored status with freshly derived; if different, update and return correct value, log inconsistency. Use Serena to find deriveBatchStatus and recalculateCharacterBatchSummary before writing.

title: M1: Add startup cleanup for orphaned preview characters
description: Temporary characters with lifecycle_status='preview' are created during batch preview rendering and deleted after image ingest. Deletion is best-effort (catch(() => {})). Failed deletions leave orphan preview characters in the DB indefinitely. Fix: on Casting Room mount (CastingPipelinePanel.jsx), add a useEffect that fetches characters with lifecycleStatus=preview, filters to those older than 1 hour, and fires best-effort DELETE for each. Alternatively add server-side cleanup on dev server startup in vite.config.js where DB is initialised. Use Serena to check if GET /api/characters supports filtering by lifecycleStatus; add it if not (one-line WHERE clause addition, trivial once H4 shadow columns exist).

title: AB7: 'Open in Casting Room' — set character as Active from Actor Bank
description:
There is currently no cross-tab communication between the Actor Bank and the Casting Room. A user who finds a character in the Actor Bank and wants to work on their portfolio must manually navigate to the Casting Room tab and find the same character in the Active Character dropdown.

**What to build:**
- Add an 'Open in Casting Room' button to ActorDetail's topBar actions.
- Clicking it: (1) switches the active tab to 'pipeline' (the Casting Room), (2) sets selectedCharacterId to this character's id.
- Implementation: ActorBankView needs to accept an onOpenInCastingRoom(characterId) callback prop, passed down from App.jsx. App.jsx already manages activeTab and selectedCharacterId (in CastingPipelinePanel) — wire the callback to set both.
- Note: selectedCharacterId is currently local state inside CastingPipelinePanel, not in App.jsx. This may require lifting it to App.jsx OR passing a ref/callback into CastingPipelinePanel. Evaluate the lightest approach.

**Why:** This is the critical cross-tab bridge. Without it, Actor Bank and Casting Room are fully isolated silos. Once this exists, users can manage their full cast from the Actor Bank and jump to production in one click.

**How to apply:** ActorDetail.jsx (button), ActorBankView.jsx (callback prop), App.jsx (wire callback, manage tab switch + character selection).
close_reason: cross-tab bridge wired via jumpToCharacterId prop pattern

title: AB6: Re-queue portfolio from Actor Bank when portfolio_failed
description:
When a character has lifecycle_status='portfolio_failed' (all ComfyUI jobs failed), there is currently no way to retry from the Actor Bank. The user must go to the Casting Room, find the character in the Active Character dropdown, and click Queue Portfolio.

**What to build:**
- In ActorDetail, when lifecycleStatus === 'portfolio_failed', show a 'Re-queue portfolio' button.
- Clicking it calls the portfolio queue API (POST /api/character-portfolio-queue) with the character's id and default view angles.
- On success, update the displayed lifecycle status to 'portfolio_pending'.
- On failure, show an error message.

**Why:** portfolio_failed is a terminal error state that requires manual intervention. Users should be able to retry without leaving the Actor Bank.

**How to apply:** ActorDetail.jsx (add conditional re-queue button). API endpoint POST /api/character-portfolio-queue already exists.
close_reason: implemented

title: AB5: Sort options for Actor Bank grid
description:
The Actor Bank grid is currently sorted only by last_rendered_at (most recent render first), hardcoded. There is no way to sort differently.

**What to build:**
- Add a sort selector to the filter bar (ActorBankFilters.jsx): 'Recent renders' (current default), 'Recently created', 'A–Z by name'.
- Pass sortBy param to GET /api/characters (the API already accepts sortBy=last_rendered_at; add support for sortBy=created_at and sortBy=name).
- Back-end: extend the GET /api/characters handler in vite.config.js to handle the two new sortBy values.

**Why:** Users with large Actor Banks need to find characters efficiently. Name-sorted and creation-sorted views are basic navigation needs.

**How to apply:** ActorBankFilters.jsx (add sort dropdown), ActorBankView.jsx (pass sortBy), vite.config.js GET /api/characters handler (add sort cases).
close_reason: implemented

title: AB4: Image keep/discard actions in Actor Bank gallery
description:
ActorDetail shows a strip of reference images but they are display-only. Users cannot approve (Keep) or reject (Discard) images from the Actor Bank — they must go to the Casting Room's Gallery section.

**What to build:**
- Add Keep / Discard buttons under each image in the ActorDetail image strip.
- Approved images show a green checkmark indicator; rejected images show muted/faded state (or are hidden with a 'show rejected' toggle).
- Keep calls POST /api/generated-image-approve with the image id.
- Discard calls POST /api/generated-image-reject.
- Image review state is already in the images array returned by GET /api/characters?id=<id> (reviewStatus field in payload_json).

**Why:** Image curation is a core part of Actor Bank management. The current read-only gallery is not actionable. Users curate their reference images to ensure only the best views are used when the character feeds into pv9 (Prompt Builder integration).

**How to apply:** ActorDetail.jsx image strip section. API endpoints already exist.
close_reason: implemented

title: AB3: Inline rename + archive/restore in Actor Bank detail
description:
ActorDetail currently only has a Delete action. Basic management actions (rename, archive, restore) are available in the Casting Room's Active Character panel but not surfaced in the Actor Bank.

**What to build:**
- **Rename:** Click character name in detail header → inline text input → Enter to save, Escape to cancel. Calls POST /api/character-rename. Pattern already exists in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — replicate it.
- **Archive:** If character is active, show 'Archive' button in topBar actions. Calls POST /api/character-archive. On success, call onBack() and trigger reload.
- **Restore:** If character is archived (archived_at is set), show 'Restore' button instead. Calls POST /api/character-restore.

**Why:** Users should not need to go to the Casting Room to rename or archive a character. These are basic record-management actions that belong in the character's detail view.

**How to apply:** ActorDetail.jsx (add rename state + archive/restore buttons). No new API endpoints needed — /api/character-rename, /api/character-archive, /api/character-restore already exist.
close_reason: inline rename, archive, restore all wired up in ActorDetail

title: Epic: Actor Bank — full UI implementation
description:
The Actor Bank tab currently shows a read-only grid of characters with filters and a detail view that only offers Delete. It is missing lifecycle visibility, management actions (rename, archive/restore), image curation (keep/discard), portfolio re-queue, and the cross-tab bridge that unlocks pv9.

This epic tracks the full build-out from read-only browser to a complete character management surface. Completion of this epic unblocks qwen-prompt-builder-pv9 (Prompt Builder ↔ Actor Bank integration).

**Why:** The Actor Bank is the long-term home for all cast characters. Without lifecycle visibility and management actions, users must switch to the Casting Room to do anything — making the Actor Bank a dead end rather than a useful hub.

**How to apply:** Use this epic to group and sequence the individual Actor Bank issues below. Close this epic when all child issues are closed and pv9 is unblocked.
close_reason: All AB1-AB7 child issues completed in P6; Actor Bank is fully implemented with lifecycle badges, image curation, sort, archive/restore, re-queue, and cross-tab bridge. pv9 also complete.

title: Rewrite PROJECT_CONTEXT.md — missing 3 of 4 application tabs entirely
description:
PROJECT_CONTEXT.md describes the application as a prompt assembly + polish tool with a 3-layer architecture. It does not mention: the Casting Room tab, the Character Builder tab, the Actor Bank tab, the SQLite database, ComfyUI integration, Chroma vector system, or the character lifecycle. The entire document is oriented around the polish flow only.

The document is short (199 lines) and should be completely rewritten as a concise but complete architectural overview covering all four tabs, their dependency relationships, all external services, and the key subsystems.

**Why:** This is the document new contributors and AI agents read for project context. Missing 75% of the application makes it actively misleading.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All four tabs described
- Full dependency chain explained
- External services listed with failure modes
- Key subsystems identified (assembler, polish, lifecycle, batch pipeline, vector/similarity)

title: Arch: migrate saved prompts and workspace profiles from localStorage to SQLite
description: Prompt Builder currently stores saved prompts (30-item cap), prompt history (12 entries), and workspace profiles in localStorage. This is inconsistent with the Casting Room which moved session state to SQLite. localStorage data is silently wiped by browser data clear, is browser-specific (breaks across profiles/devices), and the 30-item cap exists due to storage constraints that disappear with a database. Requires: GET/POST /api/saved-prompts and GET/POST /api/profiles API endpoints + DB schema. Prompt history (genuinely ephemeral) can remain in localStorage.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: director blend adds secondary light/color chips causing validation conflicts
description: blendPresetChips (App.jsx) takes all chips from the dominant director and appends the first chip from each dimension of the secondary director. For single-source dimensions (light, color, shot) this produces two chips — e.g. two light chips — which immediately triggers the light-discipline validation warning, making blending feel broken. Fix: for dimensions where the dominant already has chips, do not append the secondary chip. Or apply a post-blend single-chip resolution for light/shot dimensions.
close_reason: Closed

title: M3: Add scope indicator and count to Clean up button
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

The 'Clean up' button that archives preview characters gives no indication of how many characters it will affect or from which scope. After several batch sessions with previews, a user won't know if they're archiving 2 or 20 characters.

NOTE: This issue may become unnecessary if C2 is implemented (Generate Previews will no longer create character records). Evaluate after C2 is complete — close this as won't-fix if the Clean up button no longer exists.

## What Needs to Be Done (if button still exists after C2)

1. Update button label dynamically: 'Clean up (6 preview characters from this session)' or 'Clean up (14 preview characters — all sessions)'

2. Add a confirmation dialog before executing that lists the character names to be archived

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on the Clean up button in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx to find its current implementation and where it reads the preview character count.

title: M2: Replace per-save Portfolio toast with batch Portfolio action
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

The 'Generate Full Portfolio?' toast fires after Save to Cast in Path B, interrupting the user mid-review before they have finished evaluating all candidates. It causes a premature portfolio decision for one character while several more candidates remain unreviewed.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Remove the toast trigger from the Save to Cast handler (handleCandidateAction, 'save' branch)

2. Add a persistent 'Queue Portfolios (N)' button above the Active Character dropdown that:
   - Appears when at least one character has been saved from the current batch
   - Disappears when all saved characters already have portfolios queued
   - N = count of saved characters without a portfolio
   - Clicking it queues portfolios for all of them at once using currently selected workflow and default view angles

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'handleCandidateAction' in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, specifically the 'save' branch where the toast is triggered. Use find_symbol on 'queueCharacterPortfolio' to understand how to batch-queue.
close_reason: Post-save toast removed; Queue Portfolios (N) button added above Active Character section for batch portfolio queue

title: M1: Fix Approve+Portfolio hidden dependency on view angle pre-selection
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

'Approve + Portfolio' silently degrades to a toast if no view angles are pre-selected in the Portfolio section. View angle selection lives below the candidate cards. A user clicking 'Approve + Portfolio' during review has almost certainly not scrolled down to configure view angles yet. Getting a toast reads as a failure, not a shortcut.

## What Needs to Be Done

Implement Option A (recommended): 'Approve + Portfolio' always queues all 6 default views when no angles are pre-selected. Show a one-time dismissible note: 'Queued all 6 views. Adjust in Portfolio section to change defaults.'

Remove the 'Generate Full Portfolio?' toast that this action currently triggers (the toast fires when no workflow is set — verify with Serena, may be a separate condition).

Default 6 view angles: front_portrait, three_quarter, profile, full_body, audition_still, cinematic_scene.

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'handleApproveAndQueuePortfolio' in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx to read the current logic and identify where the toast is triggered and where view angle selection is checked.
close_reason: handleApproveAndQueuePortfolio now defaults to all 6 views; only shows toast when no workflow selected

title: Reset batchPreviewJobs and batchPreviewImages when selected batch changes
description:
Why: batchPreviewJobs and batchPreviewImages are keyed by candidateId and never cleared when selectedBatchId changes. If the user switches to a different batch, stale preview state from the previous batch is still in memory. While UUIDs make ID collisions unlikely, the preview counter ('N/M previews ready') and Generate Previews button disabled logic will be wrong for the new batch.

What needs to be done:
- In the useEffect that watches selectedBatchId (or wherever candidates are cleared on batch change), also reset:
    setBatchPreviewJobs({})
    setBatchPreviewImages({})
- Verify that any in-flight audit poll jobs for the old batch's previews don't write into the new batch's state after the reset.

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — find the selectedBatchId useEffect that calls refreshBatch or resets candidates.
close_reason: Reset batchPreviewJobs and batchPreviewImages in the selectedBatchId useEffect before refreshBatch fires.

title: Enable 'More Takes' on rejected audition results
description:
Why: Currently More Takes is only useful before a decision is made. But a user may reject an audition result and then change their mind — they want more takes on that character without re-running the full audition.

What needs to be done:

- Add a note in the UI near the More Takes section for rejected results: 'This candidate was rejected — you can still generate more images.'
- Verify that handleMoreTakes(characterId, selectedViews) works correctly regardless of the audition status (it only needs characterId and views — the audition status is irrelevant to the queue operation).
- No backend changes needed.

Key file: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — search for MoreTakesPanel usage in the render section to find the gate condition.

title: Unified CharacterCard component for audition, batch review, and active character sections
description:
Why: Audition results, batch candidates, and the active character section each render character data with different custom markup. There is no shared component. Adding features (like a status badge or preview image) requires updating three separate places.

What needs to be done:
- Create src/components/CastingRoom/CharacterCard.jsx — a single component that renders a character's visual profile, status badges, thumbnail image, and action buttons.
- Props: character (profile data), auditionId (optional), candidateId (optional), actions (array of { label, onClick, variant }) for approve/reject/save/more-takes buttons, previewImageUrl (optional), lifecycleStatus (optional).
- Create src/components/CastingRoom/CharacterCard.module.css for styles.
- Replace the inline rendering of audition result cards in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx with CharacterCard.
- Replace inline batch candidate cards with CharacterCard.
- Replace the Active Character info display with CharacterCard.

Key files to refactor: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx (the render section, lines 650-1100+ covers all three card areas). This is a pure refactor — no behavior changes.

title: Character lifecycle status field (draft → auditioned → portfolio_pending → ready → finalized)
description:
Why: The characters table has no status field beyond archived_at. There is no way to know programmatically whether a character has been auditioned, has a portfolio pending, or is fully ready. This drives smarter UI behavior.

What needs to be done:
- Add a new DB migration in api/lib/db/schema.js MIGRATIONS array: 'ALTER TABLE characters ADD COLUMN lifecycle_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT draft'
- Valid values: draft, auditioned, portfolio_pending, ready, finalized (archived is already handled by archived_at column).
- Update api/lib/characters/schemas.js CharacterProfileSchema to include lifecycleStatus as an optional enum field.
- Update api/lib/db/repositories.js: createCharacter sets status to 'draft', updateCharacter supports patching it.
- Update runAudition() in api/lib/audition/auditionOrchestrator.js: set lifecycleStatus to 'auditioned' when creating the character.
- Update saveCandidateAsCharacter() in api/lib/characters/batchReview.js: set lifecycleStatus to 'auditioned'.
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx handleQueuePortfolio(): after queuing, call updateCharacter to set lifecycleStatus to 'portfolio_pending'.
- In handleGeneratedImageReview('approve'): if character now has approved images, set lifecycleStatus to 'ready'.
- UI: highlight characters with 'portfolio_pending' status in the Active Character dropdown (e.g. a colored dot or label).
close_reason: lifecycleStatus field added to schema, set at audition/batch-save, PATCH endpoint added, frontend updates status on queue-portfolio and image-approve, dropdown shows status indicators

title: Quick preview renders for batch pipeline candidates
description:
Why: Batch candidates (Path B) are text-only — users must save a candidate before seeing any image, making it hard to choose. Path A shows images immediately. Path B should be visually competitive.

What needs to be done:
- After handleGenerateBatch() succeeds, add a 'Generate Previews' button in the batch candidate review section.
- When clicked: for each 'accepted' candidate in the current batch, call saveBatchCandidate to promote it to a character (or create a new lightweight endpoint that runs compile+queue without fully saving), queue a front_portrait-only render via queueCharacterPortfolio with views: ['front_portrait'] and a 'quick' style option.
- Store the preview job IDs in a new batchPreviewJobs state and start the audit poller.
- Display the preview image thumbnail on each candidate card once the render completes.
- Note: this feature depends on lifecycle states (Phase 2) to mark preview characters as 'preview' status so they are distinguishable from production characters. For Phase 1, a simpler approach: add a previewCharacterId field to the candidate card state only (do not persist as a status).

Key files: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx handleGenerateBatch (line 580), saveBatchCandidate from src/lib/api/characterBatches.js, queueCharacterPortfolio from src/lib/api/portfolio.js.

title: Per-candidate loading state — replace global actionLoading
description:
Why it matters: actionLoading is a single boolean that disables ALL candidate buttons while any one candidate action is in flight. If you have 10 candidates and click Cast on one, all 10 are frozen until that action completes.

Fix: Replace global actionLoading for candidate actions with a per-candidate state: const [candidateActionId, setCandidateActionId] = useState(null). In handleCandidateAction, set setCandidateActionId(candidateId) at the start and setCandidateActionId(null) in finally. Each candidate button disables when candidateActionId === candidate.id instead of checking global actionLoading.

Note: actionLoading is also used for non-candidate actions (portfolio, rename, etc). Keep the global actionLoading for those — this change only affects the candidate button disabled props.

title: Resume Comfy polling on component re-mount for pending jobs
description: Audit and portfolio polling state lives in React component memory (auditPollRef, portfolioPollRef, auditionResults, portfolioJobs). If the user switches tabs while polling is active, the interval is cleared (or never called) and any subsequently completed Comfy jobs are not ingested. Users who navigate away and return will see 'pending' status forever and must use Developer Tools > Ingest Outputs manually — an undiscoverable recovery path. Fix: in useEffect on mount, check for non-terminal jobs (call getComfyJobsStatus for any pending auditionResults / portfolioJobs) and resume the appropriate poll. This requires persisting enough job state to survive a re-render (consider sessionStorage or an API call to re-fetch job lists).

title: L4: Confirm per-card render progress badges exist on CharacterCard
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

The RenderStatusBar shows aggregate progress (total done/total queued). It's unclear whether individual CharacterCard components also show per-card render status during active rendering. If they don't, the user can't tell which specific character is rendering at any moment.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Use Serena to confirm whether CharacterCard currently renders a status badge or image-loading indicator during active renders (check renderStatus prop handling)
2. If per-card progress exists: RenderStatusBar serves as aggregate-only context — no change needed
3. If per-card progress does NOT exist: add status badge (pending / rendering / done) to CharacterCard as part of H2 unified component work

This issue is a discovery task — it may close immediately if the behavior already exists, or it may generate a sub-task under H2.

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on 'CharacterCard' and look for renderStatus prop usage. Check whether auditionStatuses or auditionImages are passed down to individual cards in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx.
close_reason: Per-card render progress already exists: StatusBadge per view-angle (line ~1041 in CastingPipelinePanel) plus inline image display. H2 CharacterCard work doesn't need to add this.

title: L3: Prompt pack selector — default to most recent pack, add Show history toggle

## Why This Issue Exists

The prompt pack selector shows all compiled versions for each view angle. Users are almost always interested in the most recently compiled version. Older versions clutter the selector and require the user to identify which is current.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Default selected pack: most recently compiled front portrait pack (sort by compiled_at DESC, take first)
2. Filter selector to show only the most recent pack per view angle by default
3. Add 'Show history' toggle to reveal all older compiled versions

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on the prompt pack selector component in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx. Use find_referencing_symbols on 'promptPacks' to understand how packs are loaded and structured — specifically whether compiled_at or a version field is available for sorting.

title: L2: Retry logic for individually failed ComfyUI jobs
description:
## Why This Issue Exists

When individual jobs fail (not all jobs for a character), there is no automatic retry. A transient ComfyUI hiccup causes permanent render gaps with no recovery path short of a full re-queue.

## What Needs to Be Done

Inside the portfolio polling tick function, when a job reaches 'failed' terminal state:
- If retry_count < 2: automatically re-queue the job, increment retry_count, keep status as 'queued'
- If retry_count >= 2: mark as permanently failed

This pairs with H4 — after L2 exhausts retries, H4's all-failed detection triggers portfolio_failed lifecycle transition.

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on the portfolio tick function to read current terminal state handling.
close_reason: Portfolio tick retries failed jobs up to 2x via queueComfyPromptPack; allDone waits for retry exhaustion

1. Add last_rendered_at nullable TEXT column to characters table (migration)
2. Update the image ingestion path to write current timestamp to last_rendered_at whenever a new generated_image is ingested for a character
3. Use last_rendered_at to sort characters in the Actor Bank by most recent activity

## Serena Usage

Use find_symbol on the image ingestion function (likely in pollAuditTick or ingestOutputs area) to find where generated_image records are created.

title: Phase 2: 'preview' lifecycle status for batch-preview characters
description:
Why: Generate Previews (57f) calls saveBatchCandidate which creates real DB characters with lifecycleStatus='auditioned'. These preview characters are indistinguishable from intentionally saved production characters in the Active Character dropdown and the character list. A user who generates previews for 10 candidates will suddenly have 10 ghost characters cluttering their cast.

What needs to be done:
- Add 'preview' as a valid lifecycle_status value in the DB schema (api/lib/db/schema.js migration).
- In handleGeneratePreviews: after saveBatchCandidate, call patchCharacterLifecycle(characterId, 'preview') to mark it as preview-only.
- In the Active Character dropdown, filter out or visually de-emphasize characters with lifecycleStatus='preview'.
- When a preview character is formally saved (handleCandidateAction 'save'), upgrade its lifecycle status to 'auditioned'.
- In the RenderStatusBar or a separate notice, show how many preview characters exist and offer a 'Clean up previews' action that archives all with lifecycleStatus='preview'.

This is explicitly called out as Phase 2 follow-up in the 57f issue notes.
close_reason: 'preview' added to schema enum, vite middleware VALID list, and new api/character-lifecycle.js Vercel handler. handleGeneratePreviews patches lifecycle to 'preview' after save. Active Character dropdown filters preview chars out. 'N preview characters · Clean up' notice archives them on demand.

title: PHASE 3: Render priority tiers (Quick Preview > Audition > Portfolio)
description:
Why: All renders are currently treated equally. Quick preview renders (low-res, fast) should not block portfolio renders (high-res, important). Audition renders should be prioritized over batch previews.

What needs to be done:
- Add a renderPriority parameter to queueCharacterPortfolio() and queueMoreTakes() in src/lib/api/portfolio.js.
- Pass this through to the ComfyUI queue endpoint — ComfyUI supports a 'front' queue option that places jobs at the front of the queue.
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx: pass priority 'high' for audition renders, 'normal' for portfolio renders, 'low' for quick batch previews.
- Update the RenderStatusBar (from qwen-prompt-builder-853) to show priority tiers.
- Note: ComfyUI priority support is limited — investigate whether ComfyUI's API actually supports priority queuing before investing heavily here.

This is a Phase 3 item.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete

title: PHASE 3: Replace polling with WebSocket for real-time render status
description:
Why: The current setInterval polling (POLL_MS) creates unnecessary API traffic and causes lag between a render completing and the UI updating. WebSockets would give instant status updates.

What needs to be done:
- Set up a WebSocket server in the Vite dev middleware (vite.config.js) and a Vercel-compatible WebSocket or SSE handler for production.
- When a ComfyUI render completes (detected either by polling on the server side or via ComfyUI webhook), broadcast to the connected client.
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, replace startAuditPoll() and startPortfolioPoll() with a WebSocket connection that receives status updates and ingest notifications.
- Keep the setInterval polling as a fallback if WebSocket is unavailable.
- Note: This is a significant infrastructure change. Vercel serverless does not support persistent WebSocket connections — may need SSE (Server-Sent Events) instead, or a third-party channel (e.g. Ably, Pusher) for cloud deployments.

This is a Phase 3 item — do not start until Phase 1 and Phase 2 are complete.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete

Fix: add a visible 'Showing images for: [Character Name]' label in the Gallery section header, directly below the section title. This makes clear the gallery is a filtered per-character view, not a global collection. Use the savedCharacters array to resolve name from selectedCharacterId.
close_reason: Gallery subtitle changed to 'Showing images for: [Name]' when a character is selected.

title: Epic: Character management (rename and archive)
description: The Active Character dropdown accumulates characters indefinitely with no way to rename or archive them. After generating images for several characters across sessions the dropdown becomes unmanageable. Minimum viable management: rename and archive (soft-delete from dropdown). This is the foundation the Actor Bank UI from the implementation brief would build on.
close_reason: Character rename and archive implemented in Active Character section.

title: Director grid: fix overflow with 60 directors
description: The director grid uses flex-wrap which produces uneven chip widths at 60 directors. Switch to CSS grid with auto-fill and minmax(80px, 1fr) columns so the grid fills evenly at any panel width. Custom director slots (added in v2a) must continue to appear after a visual separator. Affects DirectorSection.module.css .dirGrid rule only — no logic changes needed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Saved prompts panel in output area
description: Workspace profiles (qpb_workspace_profiles_v1) cover full-state save/restore but there is no quick 'Save prompt' action on the output panel itself. Add a dedicated Save button to PromptOutput that saves the current prompt text (assembled or polished), director key, scene, and timestamp under a user-given name. Display saved entries in a collapsible panel on the right. Allow rename and delete. This is the 'explicit Save with name' piece missing from the PARTIAL 7.9 item in AGENT_HANDOFF.md.
close_reason: Saved prompts panel added to PromptOutput: Save button in header, collapsible list with copy/restore/rename/delete per entry, persisted to localStorage under qpb_saved_prompts_v1, max 30 entries. Also fixed pre-existing ChipSection  syntax corruption from earlier session.

title: L3: Document project_id columns as reserved for Production Room
description: project_id nullable columns exist on characters, prompt_packs, and generated_images tables. They are unused forward-looking placeholders for a Production Room feature. No code change needed. Fix: add a SQL comment in schema.js above each project_id column: '-- Reserved for Production Room feature (Horizon 2). Not currently used.' Add a note in the Known Gaps section of APPLICATION_REFERENCE.md explaining these columns are not populated or queried by any current code path.

title: L2: Raise custom directors cap from 3 to 10
description: customDirectors array is capped at 3 entries while the built-in set has 61 directors. Users working with niche directors not in the built-in set are unnecessarily constrained. Fix: find saveCustomDirector in App.jsx and change the cap from 3 to 10 (or remove it entirely if there is no layout concern). If the director chip grid has layout issues with more entries, that is a CSS problem to solve with auto-fill/minmax — not a reason to cap custom directors.

title: L1: Add Vitest suite for assembler.js
description: The assembler (src/utils/assembler.js) is a pure function with 29 REWRITES entries and three deduplication checks. No tests exist. Install Vitest if not present (npm i -D vitest). Create src/utils/__tests__/assembler.test.js. Tests to cover: each REWRITE trigger independently (29 cases), full assembly with known inputs verifying fragment order, deduplication (one test per check type: exact match, substring, overlap), edge cases (empty scene, empty chips, all defaults, no director, no scenario), cascade rewrite (REWRITE #1 output matches REWRITE #2 trigger), @slug expansion, getCharDesc for all gender x age combinations.

value: Qwen Prompt Builder (Prompt-o-matic): Cinematic prompt assembly tool for Qwen image generation. Stack: Vite+React, Node API, optional Tauri desktop. 60 directors with interaction scenarios. Features AI polish via Claude/Ollama/LM Studio, character builder, casting pipeline, ComfyUI integration. Key files: App.jsx (state), assembler.js (prompt assembly), polishCore.js (LLM integration). Architecture is 3-layer: React frontend, Node API, domain logic core. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md, PROJECT_CONTEXT.md, .cursorrules for detailed context.
